differentials.raw: symptomatic for months to years prior to treatment

differentials.raw: mild joint pain, tenderness, swelling (joint effusion)

differentials.name: Ischemic necrosis of femoral head
differentials.raw: Ischemic necrosis of femoral head (may be indistinguishable, more irregular configuration)

differentials.name: Giant cell tumor
differentials.raw: Giant cell tumor (usually larger + less well demarcated, not calcified, older age group with closed growth plate)

differentials.name: Langerhans cell histiocytosis
differentials.raw: Langerhans cell histiocytosis (less well-defined, variable margins)

context: =  CODMAN TUMOR = BENIGN CHONDROBLASTOMA = CARTILAGE-CONTAINING GIANT CELL TUMOR =  benign cartilaginous tumor with predilection for growing skeleton Frequency:   1% of primary bone neoplasms (700 cases in world literature) Age:   peak in 2nd decade (range, 8-59 years); 10-26 years (90%); M÷F = 2÷1; occurs before cessation of enchondral bone growth Path:   derived from primitive cartilage cells Histo:   polyhedral chondroblasts + reactive multinucleated giant cells + nodules of pink amorphous material (= chondroid) = epiphyseal chondromatous giant cell tumor (resembles chondromyxoid fibroma);
